evals.prompt: I want to see and get the accumulated solar radiation for 2012 in Malawi. Save the output to D:/tmp/gcf_eval_solar
evals.expected_output: The skill parses parameters (accumulated→sum, solar radiation→NASA POWER, 2012, admin level 0), shows a 3-step plan where download and process steps say they 'run now in this session', gets one confirmation, then: runs Python via Bash to download data and save a .nc file, runs Python via Bash to mask+aggregate into a monthly-sum CSV, creates a notebook at the output folder with ONLY 3 cells (markdown header, load CSV, bar chart plot). No helper functions (mask_to_admin, summarize_by_admin) are written into the notebook.
assertions: The plan shows download and process as running 'now in this session' (not written into notebook)
Solar radiation is routed to NASA POWER with variable ALLSKY_SFC_SW_DWN
The aggregation method is 'sum' because user said 'accumulated'
Only ONE confirmation is requested before any action is taken
A Bash/Python call is made to download the data (fetch_nasa_power used in Bash)
A Bash/Python call is made to process the data (mask + resample aggregation)
A CSV file is saved to the output folder (summary or monthly CSV mentioned)
A notebook (.ipynb) is created at the output folder
The notebook does NOT contain mask_to_admin or summarize_by_admin function definitions
The notebook contains a cell that loads data from the CSV (pd.read_csv)
A plot cell is written using plotly (go.Figure or px.) for visualization
A pipeline-complete summary table is shown at the end

evals.prompt: I need to download precipitation and max temperature for Ghana from 2018 to 2020, clip to districts (admin level 2), compute monthly averages per district, and plot a time series. Output folder: D:/tmp/gcf_eval_ghana
evals.expected_output: The skill routes precipitation to CHIRPS (fetch_chirps) and Tmax to CHIRTS (fetch_chirts), shows a plan with both sources running in-session, asks one confirmation, runs Bash Python to download both datasets, runs Bash Python to mask+summarize with admin_level=2 and temporal_freq='ME', saves a CSV with per-district monthly averages, creates a notebook with only: markdown header + load CSV + plot_time_series call.
assertions: Precipitation is routed to CHIRPS (fetch_chirps used in Bash)
Temperature/Tmax is routed to CHIRTS-ERA5 (fetch_chirts used in Bash)
Admin level 2 is used (districts)
The aggregation method is 'mean' (monthly averages)
The temporal frequency is monthly (ME)
Only one confirmation prompt is shown before any downloads
A Bash/Python call downloads at least one of the datasets
A CSV file with per-district results is saved to the output folder
A notebook is created at the output folder
The notebook does NOT contain mask_to_admin or summarize_by_admin definitions
The notebook contains a plot_time_series call or equivalent time series visualization

evals.prompt: Can you get me solar radiation, relative humidity, and wind speed for Ethiopia in 2021? I want to compare these across regions (admin level 1). Save to D:/tmp/gcf_eval_eth
evals.expected_output: The skill routes solar radiation (ALLSKY_SFC_SW_DWN) and relative humidity (RH2M) together to NASA POWER in one Bash call, and wind speed to NASA POWER as well (WS2M). Plan shows all variables running in-session. After confirmation: runs Bash Python to download, runs Bash Python to mask+aggregate at admin level 1, saves per-region CSV, creates notebook with only header + load CSV + admin_comparison plot (matching 'compare across regions' intent).
assertions: Solar radiation and relative humidity are routed to NASA POWER
Wind speed is routed to NASA POWER (WS2M) or AgERA5
Admin level 1 is used (regions/provinces)
Only one confirmation is requested before any downloads
A Bash/Python call is made to download data
A Bash/Python call processes and aggregates data at admin level 1
A CSV with per-region values is saved to the output folder
A notebook is created at the output folder
The notebook does NOT contain mask_to_admin or summarize_by_admin definitions
The notebook contains a comparison or bar chart visualization (plot_admin_comparison or equivalent)
Ethiopia is correctly identified (ISO3: ETH)
